items.summary: For more than 60 years, Frank Pritchard has diligently checked the weather, adding to a record that stretches back more than a century. But he's part of a quietly shrinking network of observers around Australia. Without it, scientists say we risk missing out on important information that could help improve weather forecast models and climate projections.

items.title: NDIS unregistered provider crackdown coming, as Butler searches for more than $6b in savings
items.summary: NDIS Minister Mark Butler will announce reforms to the disability scheme on Wednesday that will aim to save more than $6 billion a year by 2035 — and the largely unregulated market of NDIS providers is at the top of his reform list.

items.title: As the world races to shore up oil supplies, Japan promotes a stockpiling model to Asia
items.summary: Japan has oil stockpile sites littered around the country, and as the world faces a fuel crisis, neighbours in Asia have been turning to Tokyo for assistance.

items.title: Why Angus Taylor's criticism of high immigration is raising eyebrows
items.summary: Angus Taylor's criticism of high immigration puts a strange spotlight on his own party's policy legacy.

items.title: How the perfect mother myth was unravelled by a family mystery in Italy
items.summary: I traced my family's maternal line from a home for abandoned children in Italy to my own wheelchair, confronting the myths we cling to about what makes a good mother — and the damage they do.

items.title: Today in history, April 19: Good News Week premiered 30 years ago
items.summary: On April 19, 1996, comedy news game show Good News Week premiered, hosted by Paul McDermott, sparking nine seasons, a Gold Logie nomination and some first-chances for comedians.

items.title: Jessie Ware on how cowboys, sex and parenting shaped new album Superbloom
items.summary: Whether making opulent, grown-up disco-pop or doing school pick-up, the glam British artist is having more fun than ever.

items.title: Ready for Hard Quiz success? It's time to temper those expectations
items.summary: Righteo champ, it's time to put down your toast and impress with your trivia prowess. Let's play, HARD!

items.title: Wallaroos beaten by USA in Pacific Four Series after Kansas City storm delay
items.summary: Australia loses 33-12 to the USA Eagles in their Pacific Four Series after storms in Kansas City delay kick-off by almost three hours.

items.title: Alleged arson attacks and shootings in Sydney prompt fears of escalating violence
items.summary: NSW Police has expressed concern about escalating violence between Sydney's organised crime networks, following several alleged arson attacks and public shootings overnight.

items.title: Dowerin buzzing as Lance 'Buddy' Franklin returns to footy for childhood club
items.summary: Lance Franklin has pulled on the boots to help the Dowerin Tigers, where he played his formative footy, raise much-needed funds for lights and facilities.

items.title: Power price hikes threaten Northern Territory councils, may cut access to services like pools and street lights
items.summary: Power price hikes are expected to be passed onto local councils by the Northern Territory government in the coming month, with Darwin's lord mayor warning some community services could be cut as a result.

items.title: Australian tea tree oil industry faces uncertainty over EU ban proposal
items.summary: The EU is considering banning Australian tea tree oil after a study raised concerns about potential reproductive risks, a move industry leaders warn could devastate exports and trigger global impacts.

items.title: Mushroom murderer's estranged husband Simon Patterson writing memoir
items.summary: Simon Patterson, who claims his murderous ex-wife tried to poison him on several occasions, is pursuing a book deal, his media manager has announced.

items.title: Haas expected to miss several weeks as Broncos count cost of brave win
items.summary: Brisbane's horror run of injuries has continued with Payne Haas limping off in the first half of the Broncos' one-point win over Wests Tigers.

items.title: Crows sensationally down Saints after emotional scenes at Adelaide Oval
items.summary: The Crows hold on by one point in a thriller at Adelaide Oval, as the club pays tribute to grieving skipper Jordan Dawson.

items.title: Skip the takeaway. This halloumi wrap is quicker and healthy too - ABC News
items.summary: The only cooking in this simple dinner is the halloumi. It has takeaway vibes and just the right balance of flavours to become a family favourite.

items.title: How Maltese migrants united to shape Mackay's sugarcane legacy
items.summary: Long before they were cast in bronze, a group of Maltese migrants quietly reshaped who would own farmland across north Queensland.

items.title: Death of Sandipan Dhar at Joondalup Health Campus was 'probably preventable'
items.summary: The death of a toddler at one of Perth's major hospitals was "probably preventable" and the failure to perform a blood test on him was "more than a missed opportunity", a coroner finds.

items.title: Agricultural, transport industries call for urgent road repairs in flood-hit Northern Territory
items.summary: Workers in road-reliant industries are calling on the federal government to provide urgent funding to repair the NT's road network, which was severely damaged by floods.

items.title: Social croquet connects communities as clubs record jump in numbers
items.summary: Golf croquet helps isolated elderly people reconnect with others through social competition, morning tea and laughs.

items.title: The ancient origins behind wreath laying on Anzac Day
items.summary: A remembrance tradition that Australians have adapted to commemorate fallen soldiers has ancient Greek and Roman origins.

items.title: Marie-Louise Eta takes charge of Union Berlin as first female head coach in Europe's big five leagues
items.summary: Amid a storm of misogynistic criticism, Marie-Louise Eta will become the first woman to take charge of a men's football team in one of Europe's top five leagues. But she just wants everyone to focus on the football.

items.title: Do acupuncture and herbs actually help with menopause symptoms?
items.summary: Complementary therapies may help some menopausal symptoms, but more high-quality research is needed to understand how effective and safe these treatments truly are.

items.title: FIFA World Cup fans set for $200 train tickets for 15 minute trip to MetLife Stadium
items.summary: A 15-minute round-trip train fare from New York to New Jersey's MetLife Stadium will set World Cup football fans back more than $200 as officials struggle to cover rising costs.

items.title: Producer backs pitch for major film studio in Port Kembla
items.summary: As the New South Wales government searches for a site for a second Sydney film studio, one of Australia's most prominent producers suggests a regional location is the ideal fit.

items.title: Frustrated parents of under-16s look for help to enforce 'ineffective' social media ban
items.summary: Tech companies are coming under fire as Australian parents increasingly voice concerns that some online platforms are failing to keep kids under 16 off their platforms.

items.title: Lime scooters and e-bikes have arrived in Canberra. Here's what that means for commuters
items.summary: With Neuron's orange scooters having left Canberra, Lime has introduced a fleet of green scooters and e-bikes across the capital in partnership with the ACT government.

items.title: How one walk could spark a national conversation on Australia's past

items.title: 'High-octane world' of drifting told through orchestral music in Adelaide
items.summary: Motorsport and orchestral music are coming together in a premiere in Adelaide this weekend.

items.title: Missing votes bungle caps off disastrous poll for SA's electoral commission
items.summary: The 2026 state election has been a reputational disaster for South Australia's electoral authority. Its blunder in Narungga was the "cherry on top".

items.title: How artificial intelligence is changing business interaction with customers
items.summary: Businesses say they are using AI to speed up service delivery and improve the customer experience, but economists warn this may be the "calm before the storm".

items.title: Calls for more awareness of functional neurological disorder to reduce diagnosis delays
items.summary: People with functional neurological disorder say they experience misdiagnosis and struggles within the medical system.

items.title: 'Derek' the dingo spotted roaming in Eastside
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Lone dingo 'Derek' a concern for some as he roams suburbs in Alice Springs
items.summary: An lone dingo, affectionately known as Derek, has been spotted in suburban streets, backyards and even in dog parks, raising some concern for Alice Springs residents.

items.title: Coorong fishers deploy 'ninja tactics' to outsmart fish-stealing seals
items.summary: Commercial fishers question their future as they compete with ravenous long-nosed seals that continue to rip man-sized holes in fish nets in South Australia's Coorong.

items.title: Australia banks on Ghost Bat and Ghost Shark in drone warfare shift
items.summary: Australia is on a push to keep pace with the rapid evolution of drone warfare, with an emphasis on locally produced Ghost Bat and Ghost Shark systems.

items.title: Labor considering ways to spare new homes from capital gains tax changes
items.summary: The possible budget centrepiece is likely to be finalised much closer to the May deadline than usual this year given global uncertainty, but progress has been made in recent weeks.

items.title: Why global stocks keep surging despite economic impact of Iran war
items.summary: The Australian Securities Exchange is within striking distance of new records as economists issue worst-case warnings of a close call for a global recession.

items.title: Video of police officer restraining 13yo girl on Perth train sparks excessive force probe
items.summary: Video of an off-duty police officer restraining a 13yo girl on a Perth train has sparked accusations of excessive force.

items.title: Royal tour to brand tour: Harry and Meghan seek duty and dollars in Australia
items.summary: Mixing philanthropy, influencing and brand building, Harry and Meghan’s Australian tour revives a question Queen Elizabeth once shut down: can the 'half in, half out' royal life work?

items.title: As Iran war fallout spreads, the focus shifts to Trump's mind — as it should
items.summary: The world is stuck watching a performative series of announcements about meetings that could, may, will happen, involving a president not dealing with the reality that he is in a weak and confused bargaining position.

items.title: Iran war and energy shock make delivering the federal budget unusually hard
items.summary: The budget's usual timing on the second Tuesday of May is an artifice of political convention. This year's edition just happens to be coinciding with the thunderclap of history in flux.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of the Big Day Out, footy fairytales and the circles of hell
items.summary: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

items.title: Millennials and gen Z clash in season two of Beef, starring Carey Mulligan and Oscar Isaac
items.summary: The dark comedy series returns with a biting look at generational conflict, starring Carey Mulligan and Oscar Isaac as a "combustible" middle-aged couple.

items.title: Parents of Aboriginal teen share anguish during sentence hearing of convicted police officer
items.summary: The parents of 16-year-old Jai Kalani Wright, who died after colliding with a police car, share their emotional testimonies in court as the sentencing hearing begins.

items.title: Cigno Australia, BSF Solutions and directors fined $7 million over payday lending scheme
items.summary: The fines were issued against an operation that breached credit rules and generated more than $91 million in fees and charges.

items.title: Bulldogs cop injury carnage on 'traumatic' night as superstar Darcy tears ACL
items.summary: The Bulldogs are trounced by 75 points and lose Sam Darcy to a knee injury as Sydney retains its supremacy against GWS.

items.title: Supreme Court hears murder accused Andrew Mungatopi called for help after stabbing sistergirl
items.summary: The partner of a man charged with the stabbing murder of a Tiwi Islands sistergirl tells the NT Supreme Court she saw him run outside "like a flash" before crying and repeatedly calling out "call the police".

items.title: Albanese joins meeting of leaders to talk about reopening the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Anthony Albanese has joined a virtual meeting of world leaders to discuss how the Strait of Hormuz can be secured after the conflict in the Middle East ends.

items.title: Pacific leaders declare emergency over potential fuel shortages
items.summary: The prime minister of Solomon Islands says Pacific leaders have declared an emergency over the looming threat of fuel shortages, while the Australian government contemplates a plan which could see stocks of diesel held by the US military sent to Pacific nations suffering shortages.

items.title: Panthers win golden point thriller but lose key man Kenny
items.summary: Penrith have paid a heavy price for their extra time win over the Dolphins with hooker Mitch Kenny facing a lengthy stint on the sidelines.

items.title: Viva Geelong refinery fire won't be a problem for us, Tasmanian government says
items.summary: Tasmania has 100 days of petrol and 58 days of diesel supply available, the government says — and that the Geelong refinery fire won't be a problem.

items.title: Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers plead for government support as final payday looms
items.summary: Tasmanian smelter workers at risk of losing their salaries within the next two weeks are pleading with the state and federal governments to step in — pointing to large bailouts given to other companies.

items.title: Mental health nurse Adam McGovern jailed for sexually abusing 14-year-old patient
items.summary: A former West Australian mental health nurse is sentenced to 12 years in jail after being convicted for persistent sexual abuse of a teenager and making "upskirting" videos of women and children.

items.title: Government advisory group recommends delaying NDIS 'robo-planning' changes
items.summary: The federal government has been urged by its own NDIS advisory group to delay the rollout of a controversial new way of determining support plans for participants.

items.title: Queensland government investigating possible new oil refinery in Gladstone
items.summary: As the fuel crisis continues, a proposal for an $11 billion new oil refinery in central Queensland has been put forward.

items.title: Victorian political donations website goes dark after High Court struck out election law
items.summary: Victoria has been left without laws to govern party donation disclosures — exposing the state's politics to a flood of unchecked donations.

items.title: Makers of As Deep as the Grave defend use of AI-generated Val Kilmer in movie
items.summary: A trailer for a film with an AI-generated character in the likeness of the late Val Kilmer has been slammed by social media users, but the filmmakers are defending their work.

items.title: 'Too big to ignore' housing problem top priority for productivity commissioner
items.summary: Productivity Commissioner Danielle Wood says Australia will not meet the federal government's 1.2 million new home target, as residential construction rates fall.

items.title: Narungga votes that went missing see margin increase for One Nation
items.summary: One Nation has retained the South Australian seat of Narungga, following a count of ballots that were missed on election day.

items.title: National Indigenous Cricket Championships offers pathways for young players
items.summary: The National Indigenous Cricket Championships, held in Mackay for the second year, has become one of the key development pathways for young talent.

items.title: Prince Harry and Meghan meet Bondi terror attack survivors during Sydney leg of Australian tour
items.summary: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex spoke with lifeguards who responded to the December 14 Bondi Beach terror attack and survivors.

items.title: By-election called for seat of Stafford in Brisbane's north after Jimmy Sullivan's death
items.summary: Former Member for Stafford Jimmy Sullivan was found dead at his home earlier this month, after police were called to reports of a non-suspicious death.

items.title: St Kilda to appeal suspension of Lance Collard for using homophobic slur in VFL match
items.summary: St Kilda is appealing the nine-match ban given to Lance Collard for uttering a homophobic comment, saying the young forward maintains his innocence.

items.title: Before the timer reaches zero, 100 bombs have dropped
items.summary: The Israel Defense Forces labelled it Operation Eternal Darkness. Inside Lebanon, it's now called Black Wednesday. ABC NEWS Verify has geolocated the explosions across 10 minutes of chaos.

items.title: Jet fuel shortage could see major flight disruptions in Europe, price hikes and cancellations globally
items.summary: Europe could experience major flight disruptions within weeks as global energy officials warn the continent has "maybe six weeks of jet fuel left", which could lead to further price hikes and cancellations for Australian travellers.
